dees: noun. plural of dee, the name of the letter D.
del: noun. The symbol ∇ used to denote the gradient operator.
den: noun. A small cavern or hollow place in the side of a hill, or among rocks; especially, a cave used by a wild animal for shelter or
dene: noun. A valley, especially the deep valley of a stream or rivulet.
eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
eld: noun. One's age, age in years, period of life.
elds: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of eld
else: adjective. Other; in addition to previously mentioned items.
end: noun. The terminal point of something in space or time.
ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
ess: noun. The name of the Latin script letter S / s.
led: verb. simple past and past participle of lead
lee: noun. A protected cove or harbor, out of the wind.
lees: noun. The sediment that settles during fermentation of beverages, consisting of dead yeast and precipitated parts of the fruit.
lend: verb. To allow to be used by someone temporarily, on condition that it or its equivalent will be returned.
lends: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of lend
lens: noun. An object, usually made of glass, that focuses or defocuses the light that passes through it.
lensed: adjective. Having a (specified form of) lens
lessee: noun. An individual or a corporation who has the right of use of something of value, gained through a lease agreement with the real owner
lessen: verb. To make less; to diminish; to reduce.
lessened: verb. simple past and past participle of lessen
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
need: noun. A requirement for something; something needed.
needle: noun. A long, thin, sharp implement usually for piercing as in sewing, embroidery, acupuncture, tattooing, body piercing, medical
needs: adverb. Either directly or indirectly preceded or followed by an auxiliary verb, often must: of necessity or need; necessarily,
ness: noun. A promontory; a cape or headland. (Frequently used as a suffix in placenames.)
see: verb. To perceive or detect someone or something with the eyes, or as if by sight.
seed: noun. Any propagative portion of a plant which may be sown, such as true seeds, seed-like fruits, tubers, or bulbs.
seeled: verb. simple past and past participle of seel
seels: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of seel
sees: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of see
sel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Selkup.
send: verb. To make something (such as an object or message) go from one place to another (or to someone).
sends: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of send
sense: noun. Any of the manners by which living beings perceive the physical world: for humans sight, smell, hearing, touch, taste.
sensed: verb. simple past and past participle of sense
sled: noun. A small, light vehicle with runners, used recreationally, mostly by children, for sliding down snow-covered hills; no draft animal
sned: noun. A cut, a cutting; a slash, a slight wound; a lopping or pruning.
sneds: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of sned
deen: noun. religion; religiosity; the way of life of a pious Muslim
dle: noun. A short, daily web-based guessing game or puzzle, especially one of many played one after another.
eden: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Eden (“a paradise”).
elne: verb. To strengthen; hearten; comfort; encourage.
esne: noun. A hireling of servile status; slave.
essed: verb. simple past and past participle of ess
leeses: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of leese
lendee: noun. The person to whom something is lent.
lese: verb. Alternative form of leese (“to lose”)
lsd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lishana Deni.
ned: noun. A person, usually a youth, of low social standing and education, a violent disposition and with a particular style of dress
neesed: verb. simple past and past participle of neese
neeses: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of neese
sed: verb. To edit a file or stream of text using sed.
sedes: noun. The position a word or phrase occupies within the meter.
sendee: noun. A person to whom something is sent
sneed: verb. To seethe; to become extremely frustrated and agitated.
snees: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of snee
